novel_features.name: Today's reading
novel_features.description: An opinionated public-domain book or passage to read today, rotated against what you've recently sat with.
novel_features.rationale: Requires anti-repeat against your local sit history — no catalog API picks a daily read for you.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to start a daily reading practice without deciding what to read.

novel_features.name: Sit with a passage
novel_features.description: Fetch a real Project Gutenberg passage, show an excerpt to read, and capture your reflection to your local journal.
novel_features.rationale: Requires fetching a real Gutenberg text, excerpting it, and persisting a reflection locally — no reading tracker serves the text and takes the reflection together.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for the core contemplative loop: read a real passage, write what it left you with.

novel_features.name: Reflection journal
novel_features.description: Your reflections over time, newest first — the record of your reading practice.
novel_features.rationale: Requires the local reflections table; the journal is yours, not any API's.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to read back what your reading has left you with.

novel_features.name: What to read next
novel_features.description: Ranks your want-to-read shelf against your reading history to suggest what to pick up next.
novel_features.rationale: Requires ranking your local want-shelf against your reading log — no API knows your shelf.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when you've finished a book and want the next one off your own shelf.

novel_features.name: Reading stats
novel_features.description: Your reading pace, top subjects, and rating distribution from your local log.
novel_features.rationale: Requires aggregating your local reading_log; no single API returns your personal reading stats.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to see your reading habits over time.

narrative.headline: A contemplative daily reading practice — open book APIs as your library, real public-domain passages to sit with, and a reading journal that's yours.
narrative.value_prop: Reading trackers log what you read; ebook CLIs fetch files. book-goat makes a daily practice out of real public-domain texts: today serves an opinionated pick, sit shows a real Project Gutenberg passage and takes your reflection, journal keeps them, and shelf/next/stats track your reading. Everything is local SQLite — the practice is yours.
narrative.auth_narrative: No key required. Open Library and Gutendex (Project Gutenberg) are keyless; Google Books works anonymously. Set GOOGLE_BOOKS_API_KEY only to lift the anonymous rate limit.

quickstart.comment: find a book; results flag which have free full text
an opinionated public-domain pick for today
read a real Gutenberg passage and capture a reflection
add a book to your want-to-read shelf

troubleshoots.symptom: 403 or rate-limited from Open Library / Gutendex
troubleshoots.fix: These keyless APIs allow ~1 request/second anonymously; book-goat backs off — retry, or slow bulk syncs.
Only public-domain books on Project Gutenberg have full text — use a Gutenberg id (the number in a gutendex /books result), not an Open Library id.

troubleshoots.symptom: Google Books returns 'not available in this country'
troubleshoots.fix: book-goat sends country=US by default; enrichment is optional and off the core loop.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for book-goat to keep a daily reading practice grounded in real public-domain texts — a pick to read, a passage to sit with, a reflection to keep — plus a local shelf and reading log. It is not an ebook pirate or a Goodreads mirror; it is a personal, local practice.

recipes.title: The daily loop
recipes.explanation: Get today's pick, then sit with its passage and journal a reflection.

recipes.title: Find something with free text
recipes.explanation: Search and narrow to which results have free full text.
